vitals.location: Charmey, Fribourg, Switzerland (final editions — the historical starting point for earlier editions is unconfirmed)
route_options: Long course: 114 km (sometimes listed 115 km), 1,917 m gain — Col des Mosses, Col du Pillon, Col du Mittelberg
Short course: 76 km, 1,073 m gain — Col du Mittelberg (summit ~1,633 m) as the principal climb

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 71. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 5): event confirmed defunct since 2017 (last run 2016) — these dimension scores describe an event that no longer exists and should not be read as a current recommendation. Additionally, the corrected vitals (114km/1,917m, ~1,633m high point) conflict with the stored distance=3/climbing=4/altitude=4 under the repo's dimension rubric (config/dimensions.json) — recommend a human re-check all three alongside the defunct-status question. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule; see birkebeinerrittet-road.json for precedent.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this event no longer exists. In its final 2015-2016 editions the route started and finished in Charmey, opening with a controlled descent (Descente de Bataille) before the long course climbed Col des Mosses and Col du Pillon en route to a finishing ascent of Col du Mittelberg; the short course used the Mittelberg as its one major pass. The Pringy-to-Mittelberg section, including descents, was the fully timed portion in these final editions — not a simple start-to-finish clock. The event began in 1998 as 'La Pascal Richard,' honoring the Swiss Olympic road champion, before being renamed Gruyère Cycling Tour.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and chalets available in Gruyère region; book early for events.

history.origin_story: Founded 1998 as 'La Pascal Richard,' named for the Swiss Olympic road champion, before being renamed Gruyère Cycling Tour. One of the inaugural 2011 UCI World Cycling Tour qualifying events — the program now known as the UCI Gran Fondo World Series — and listed in a 2009 UCI Golden Bike brochure among selected international cyclosportives.
notable_moments: 2011: One of the inaugural UCI World Cycling Tour (now UCI Gran Fondo World Series) qualifying events
2016 (19th edition, September 4, Charmey): final edition held, 600+ riders
2017: 20th edition cancelled — organizer cited a lack of partners and financial support
history.reputation: A well-regarded Swiss cyclosportive in its run, with real UCI World Cycling Tour pedigree from 2011. Now explicitly described by at least one Swiss cycling-route source as 'aujourd'hui défunte' — defunct today.

biased_opinion.summary: There is no current Gruyère Cycling Tour to rate. The last confirmed edition ran September 4, 2016 in Charmey — its 19th — drawing 600+ riders on a course combining Col des Mosses, Col du Pillon and Col du Mittelberg on the long route. The planned 20th edition was cancelled in 2017 when the organizer lost its partners and funding, and no edition has run since. The event does not appear in the current Swiss Cycling calendar; it was included in the inaugural 2011 UCI World Cycling Tour qualifying series, the program now known as the UCI Gran Fondo World Series, but is absent from that series' current calendar too. A third-party aggregator lists a May 2026 date in Bulle, but it has no working organizer page, no registration, and no results; treat it as stale or erroneous, not a revival.
weaknesses: This event does not currently exist — it should not be presented to riders as a bookable gran fondo
Discontinued since 2017 (last run 2016) with no revival evidence in any current official Swiss or UCI calendar
Despite the name, the final editions started and finished in Charmey, not the town of Gruyères itself
A third-party listing claiming a 2026 revival in Bulle has no supporting organizer page, registration, or results — do not trust it
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Retired since 2016-2017. If you want to ride this terrain — Col du Mittelberg, Col des Mosses, Col du Pillon — you'll need to string it together yourself; there is no organized event to enter.

final_verdict.one_liner: A defunct Swiss cyclosportive — real pre-Alpine climbing, no current event to enter.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — this event does not exist. The 2016 edition was its last; the 2017 edition was cancelled and none has run since.
final_verdict.alternatives: For similar Fribourg/pre-Alpine Swiss terrain with a currently active event, check the Swiss Cycling calendar (swiss-cycling.ch) for current Fribourg-region cyclosportives.

San Vigilo Dolomites' (unrelated Italian gravel race, Dolomites) — neither describes this event.

rider_intel.search_text: The transcript provided does not contain race-specific content about the Gruyère Cycling Tour. Instead, it shows visitors exploring the historic town of Gruyères, which dates back to the 1500s, including visits to the Cailler Chocolate Factory and observations about the medieval château and local attractions. The speakers mention investigating the area ahead of a Tour de France stage but do not discuss gravel cycling race conditions, course features, terrain challenges, or race-day experiences specific to the Gruyère Cycling Tour event.

eligibility.notes: IDENTITY CORRECTION (2026-07-24 editorial wave 5): prior eligibility status was 'unknown' based only on an unconfirmed roadielabs.com listing for a 2026-07-12 date. codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research + curl-verification of laliberte.ch (200 OK) confirms the event is defunct: last edition 2016-09-04 (19th, Charmey, 600+ riders), 20th edition cancelled in 2017 for lack of partners/funding, no edition since, not in the 2026 Swiss Cycling calendar or current UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendar. A third-party aggregator's May 2026 Bulle listing has no organizer page, registration, or results and should be treated as stale/erroneous. Vitals rebuilt around the final documented long-course configuration (114km/1,917m) rather than the unsupported 130.4km/2,798m previously on file, which matched no verified edition.

catalog_flags.status_note: Defunct road cyclosportive (2016 final edition, 2017 cancellation confirmed) — course_description and biased_opinion rewritten to honest discontinuation framing per the birkebeinerrittet-road.json precedent.
